Second- and Third-Order Effects

  • Second Order Effect: For Wargames, fog and information become observer contexts, belief stores, visibility/occlusion, and communications graphs. Knowledge is simulation state, not UI state. This is essential because fog of war affects what players and AI can know and act on... _Evidence:_ EVC-00861 from docs/archive/conversations/_reader/by_chat/domino_engine_refactor_prompts.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Second Order Effect: The 'src' issue illustrates the reasoning. A generic 'src' root is not bad in all projects, but in Dominium it hides ownership and allowed Codex/humans to create shadow implementations. Therefore, the policy became ownership-rooted layout rather than... _Evidence:_ EVC-01345 from docs/archive/conversations/omega_xi_pi_architecture_future/dominium_omega_xi_pi_architecture_future_proofing_planning__01_human_readable_report.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: This identity explains otherwise arbitrary game mechanics: HUD, blueprints, respawn, recipes, nanobot construction, automation, quest markers, body swapping, and frontier spawn points. It also creates natural long-term goals: restore human civilisation... _Evidence:_ EVC-00260 from docs/archive/conversations/Dominium_Complete_Conversation/deterministic_solar_system_handoff_file/Dominium_Deterministic_Solar_System_Architecture__10_accompanying_detailed_summary_and_report.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: This chat developed the architecture and implementation roadmap for the Dominium/Domino project. The user provided a master starter prompt defining Domino as a deterministic, fixed-point, ISO C89 engine and Dominium as a portable C++98 product suite. The... _Evidence:_ EVC-01130 from docs/archive/conversations/architecture_codex_prompts/dominium_domino_architecture_codex_prompts__05_reader_brief.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: Phase 1 prompts, platform prompt template, renderer prompt template/vtable/softref, setup prompts, launcher prompts, tools prompts, Phase 7+ roadmap, numeric/construction/seamless transition specs. _Evidence:_ EVC-00170 from docs/archive/conversations/Dominium_Architecture_IV/Dominium_Architecture_IV__05_reader_brief.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: This chat defined a major direction for Dominium: a deterministic robotic seed-civilisation game where players are manufactured robot bodies spawned by a mothership, then expand across scientifically generated planets by surveying, mining, building... _Evidence:_ EVC-00241 from docs/archive/conversations/Dominium_Complete_Conversation/deterministic_solar_system_handoff_file/Dominium_Deterministic_Solar_System_Architecture__06_reader_brief.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: [FACT] This chat was about designing major architecture for the Dominium/Domino deterministic engine, with emphasis on advanced simulation, gameplay mechanics, user-buildable infrastructure, arbitrary placement, buildings, splines/corridors, signage... _Evidence:_ EVC-00706 from docs/archive/conversations/_reader/by_chat/advanced_simulation_infrastructure.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: The central design problem was complexity. Dominium is not a small game concept. The user wants a deterministic simulation engine that can eventually support everything from dusty bicycle lanes and miniature railways to orbital stations, drop pods... _Evidence:_ EVC-00795 from docs/archive/conversations/_reader/by_chat/dominium_architecture_ii.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: This chat was mainly about turning Dominium from a broad game idea into a deeply planned software ecosystem. The conversation moved across engine architecture, platform abstraction, renderer abstraction, install modes, launcher design, setup... _Evidence:_ EVC-00810 from docs/archive/conversations/_reader/by_chat/dominium_architecture_iv.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.
  • Third Order Effect: Later, the user expanded the horizon: Dominium should start with simple early graphics and audio, but eventually support AAA-quality graphics, spatial audio, environmental acoustics, proximity chat, AI, navigation, terrain, vehicles, economy, combat... _Evidence:_ EVC-00950 from docs/archive/conversations/_reader/by_chat/platform_renderer_api_plan.md. _Status:_ conversation advisory evidence.

Implications for Next Work

Future task packets should include these knock-on effects before they become implementation debt.

Any later task that wants to move a claim from this chapter into live authority needs source IDs, target paths, authority compatibility, queue compatibility, and validation evidence. This chapter is therefore a review map, not a permission slip.

25. Recommended Next Steps

The best next work is review and controlled promotion, not more bulk rendering or broad implementation.

Why this chapter matters. Current repo authority permits docs/evidence updates and narrow slices only. The integrated book should become the reader surface, the evidence map should become the review surface, and the full-source appendix should remain audit backup.

Current Truth

Current repo truth comes first in this chapter. Archive and conversation evidence is used to explain design intent, recurring concerns, and review candidates without promoting it.

Integrated Evidence

The current repo-backed evidence emphasizes: Foundation Lock is the repository decision gate that determines whether Dominium can leave the Foundation Lock queue and begin a first narrow governed product slice (EVC-01457). Continue narrow governed product-spine slices recorded by '.aide/queue/current.toml' (EVC-01463). Keep Queue B hardening planned but not a substitute for the blocker repair (EVC-01467). 'FOUNDATION-CLOSEOUT-01' was blocked because dependency-direction strict reported '358' violations and '38' warnings (EVC-01462).

The archive and conversation corpus add: None in this docket. Current generated decisions are user decisions, queue decisions, or deferrals (EVC-00687). 'DECISION-0006': Should this conversation-derived claim become a future review item, remain historical, or be deferred: The assistant formalized this as the Perfect Earth Calendar, later called HPC-E. The final structure is clear, though the exact leap (EVC-00691). Decision items are review prompts. The default disposition is defer unless a later explicit task opens the scope (EVC-01427). What disposition should be chosen for this unresolved claim: The user then asked whether arbitrary placement was possible or whether the system was stuck to grids. The answer established that the engine should be grid-agnostic. [FACT] The...? (EVC-00682).

The downstream implication is that: Date anchor: 2026-05-27 Australia/Melbourne Source scope: This conversation and the files created during this conversation. Purpose: This report is a human-readable companion to the structured preservation package. It explains, in ordinary (EVC-00273).

Appendix B - Decision Index

  • EVC-00012 decision: Keep target-specific project state in '.aide/memory/'; do not copy source AIDE memory, queue history, generated context, reports, route decisions, cache-key reports, Gateway/provider status reports, '.aide.local/', raw prompts, raw responses, or secrets. _Chapters:_ 4, 21, 22, 23. _Source:_ AGENTS.md.
  • EVC-00065 decision: Travel scheduling, replay, and law decisions become nondeterministic. _Chapters:_ 18, 4, 22, 3. _Source:_ docs/architecture/INVARIANTS.md.
  • EVC-01433 decision: Rendering is presentational only and MUST NOT mutate truth or enforce authority decisions. _Chapters:_ 18, 4, 20, 22. _Source:_ docs/canon/constitution_v1.md.
  • EVC-01457 decision: Foundation Lock is the repository decision gate that determines whether Dominium can leave the Foundation Lock queue and begin a first narrow governed product slice. _Chapters:_ 19, 25, 4, 22. _Source:_ docs/repo/FOUNDATION_LOCK.md.
  • EVC-00682 decision: What disposition should be chosen for this unresolved claim: The user then asked whether arbitrary placement was possible or whether the system was stuck to grids. The answer established that the engine should be grid-agnostic. [FACT] The...? _Chapters:_ 18, 19, 20, 25. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00686 decision: What disposition should be chosen for this unresolved claim: This chat was about preserving and re-grounding Dominium's architecture around a very old constitutional contract, then checking whether the current GitHub repository still foll...? _Chapters:_ 20, 5, 18, 19. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00687 decision: None in this docket. Current generated decisions are user decisions, queue decisions, or deferrals. _Chapters:_ 19, 25, 22, 23. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00689 decision: 'DECISION-0001': What disposition should be chosen for this unresolved claim: The user then asked whether arbitrary placement was possible or whether the system was stuck to grids. The answer established that the engine should be grid-agnostic. [FACT] The...? _Chapters:_ 18, 19, 20, 25. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00690 decision: 'DECISION-0003': What disposition should be chosen for this unresolved claim: Future work must define the client's allowed dependency surface and whether it can access any simulation-adjacent prediction layer.? _Chapters:_ 6, 10, 18, 19. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00691 decision: 'DECISION-0006': Should this conversation-derived claim become a future review item, remain historical, or be deferred: The assistant formalized this as the Perfect Earth Calendar, later called HPC-E. The final structure is clear, though the exact leap... _Chapters:_ 19, 25, 22, 4. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00692 decision: 'DECISION-0007': What disposition should be chosen for this unresolved claim: Before relying on this chat as project authority, a future assistant or human reviewer must confirm whether Dominium is actually intended to be simulation-heavy, whether strict...? _Chapters:_ 18, 22, 4, 12. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00693 decision: 'DECISION-0009': What disposition should be chosen for this unresolved claim: This chat was about preserving and re-grounding Dominium's architecture around a very old constitutional contract, then checking whether the current GitHub repository still foll...? _Chapters:_ 20, 5, 18, 19. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_decision/DECISION_SUMMARY_v0.md.
  • EVC-00694 decision: [INFERENCE] The conclusion was not a finalized implementation but a working architecture: each simulation domain should be deterministic, fixed-point, content-driven, versioned, replayable, and integrated through clear read/write boundaries. _Chapters:_ 9, 20, 24, 8. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_promotion/PROMOTION_WAVE_1_CANDIDATES_v0.md.
  • EVC-00695 decision: The user also made a clear versioning decision. FACT: The user wanted major.minor.patch semantic versioning for all components/packages and for the complete game package. The user explicitly did not want build numbers or build dates in... _Chapters:_ 9, 20, 5, 17. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_promotion/PROMOTION_WAVE_1_CANDIDATES_v0.md.
  • EVC-00697 decision: This context matters because the rest of the conversation happened inside those constraints. The launcher could not become a dumping ground for OS-specific behavior, sim mutation, renderer decisions, or nondeterministic game-state changes. It had to fit... _Chapters:_ 11, 6, 10, 18. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_promotion/PROMOTION_WAVE_1_CANDIDATES_v0.md.
  • EVC-00698 decision: The user then redirected the entire plan. They argued that native OS widget GUI tools already exist through Visual Studio, Xcode, etc. The project needed a cross-platform rendered tool environment that uses the same CLI, TUI, and rendered GUI systems as... _Chapters:_ 10, 6, 11, 1. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_promotion/PROMOTION_WAVE_1_CANDIDATES_v0.md.
  • EVC-00699 decision: The user later asked where to create Visual Studio and Xcode apps after opening the repo as a folder. The assistant advised that Visual Studio and Xcode projects should be generated through CMake, not hand-authored or treated as authoritative source... _Chapters:_ 12, 6, 18, 19. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_promotion/PROMOTION_WAVE_1_CANDIDATES_v0.md.
  • EVC-00703 decision: The user pasted the final README after those cleanup changes. At that point, the README had the current active form: deterministic constraints clarified, ports unified under one source hierarchy, '/ports' metadata-only if retained, Section 9 normative... _Chapters:_ 9, 18, 20, 22. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_promotion/PROMOTION_WAVE_1_CANDIDATES_v0.md.
  • EVC-01068 decision: Safe now: archive reading, decision review, promotion review, reconciliation crosswalks, docs-only microtask preparation, and validators. _Chapters:_ 18, 19, 20, 25. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/_synthesis/CURRENT_PROJECT_ATLAS_v0.md.
  • EVC-01414 decision: Use: design intent, review backlog, decision and promotion candidates _Chapters:_ 18, 19, 20, 25. _Source:_ docs/archive/docs_corpus/_archive/DOCS_CONVERSATION_CORPUS_INTEGRATION_v0.md.
  • EVC-01427 decision: Decision items are review prompts. The default disposition is defer unless a later explicit task opens the scope. _Chapters:_ 19, 25, 22, 23. _Source:_ docs/archive/docs_corpus/_reconciliation/DOCS_DECISION_DOCKET_v0.md.
  • EVC-00110 decision: The final uploaded prompt asked for this preservation package. The key thing to preserve is that most architecture proposals are recommendations, not accepted user decisions yet. The next best action is to decide which recommendations become canon, then... _Chapters:_ 12, 9, 18, 20. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/Build_and_Future_Proofing/Dominium_Build_and_Future_Proofing_Architecture__01_human_readable_report.md.
  • EVC-00117 decision: Which recommendations are decisions versus advice? _Chapters:_ 18, 19, 20, 25. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/Build_and_Future_Proofing/Dominium_Build_and_Future_Proofing_Architecture__09_in_chat_reader.md.
  • EVC-00123 decision: Chat label: Dominium Build and Future-Proofing Architecture Date anchor: 2026-05-27 Australia/Melbourne Scope: This report covers the visible conversation and the preservation task that produced the accompanying handoff files. It does not... _Chapters:_ 18, 22, 21, 4. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/Build_and_Future_Proofing/Dominium_Build_and_Future_Proofing_Architecture__10_accompanying_human_readable_detailed_summary_report.md.
  • EVC-00135 decision: DECISION-06: Calendars are pure renderers and never affect physics. [FACT] _Chapters:_ 2, 18, 19, 20. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/Chronology_Celestial_Systems/Dominium_Chronology_Celestial_Systems__05_reader_brief.md.
  • EVC-00136 decision: DECISION-12: HPC-E Year Day and Leap Day are appended after February; canonical mode is undated and compatibility mode may use Feb 29/30. [FACT / INFERENCE] _Chapters:_ 18, 20, 22, 4. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/Chronology_Celestial_Systems/Dominium_Chronology_Celestial_Systems__05_reader_brief.md.
  • EVC-00139 decision: This chat was a long architecture/specification-generation session for the Dominium Game project. It focused on turning a broad v3 game architecture into concrete, file-by-file '.txt' implementation specs for Codex automation. The user decided that Codex... _Chapters:_ 12, 1, 5, 6. _Source:_ docs/archive/conversations/Dominium_Architecture_I/Dominium_Architecture_I__05_reader_brief.md.
  • EVC-00140 decision: The visible chat generated many implementation-spec prompts across engine, platform, render, audio, systems, and the start of game.   • EVC-00001 specification: Future Series: Sigma-1, Sigma-2, Sigma-3, Sigma-4, Sigma-5, Phi, Upsilon, Zeta Replacement Target: none; later mirrors and machine-readable governance surfaces must derive from this file rather than compete with it Binding Sources... _Chapters:_ 12, 4, 18, 22. _Source:_ AGENTS.md.

  • EVC-00007 specification: express behavior composition through profiles, bundles, law surfaces, and explicit constraints _Chapters:_ 12, 1, 2, 3. _Source:_ AGENTS.md.
  • EVC-00014 specification: create or resume a bounded AIDE task before reporting blocked; ask the user only for semantic authority conflicts, destructive ambiguity, missing external secrets, or required review gates. _Chapters:_ 21, 4, 22, 23. _Source:_ AGENTS.md.
  • EVC-00015 specification: The project is about invention, production, logistics, economics, settlement, trust, communication, and institutional power emerging from lawful simulation rather than scripted outcomes. Commands, processes, packs, capabilities, diagnostics, evidence... _Chapters:_ 5, 1, 2, 3. _Source:_ README.md.
  • EVC-00017 specification: Dominium is the official game, product, and domain layer on top of Domino. It defines rules, process meaning, law targets, domain interpretation, authored content use, and product composition. _Chapters:_ 1, 6, 10, 5. _Source:_ README.md.
  • EVC-00018 specification: AIDE and Codex are repo/control-plane harnesses and bounded patch execution surfaces. They help operate the repository; they do not replace canon, contracts, validation, or evidence. _Chapters:_ 8, 3, 5, 7. _Source:_ README.md.
  • EVC-00023 specification: The active portability floor includes Windows 7 SP1, macOS 10.9.5, and Linux. C++17 language mode is allowed, but public ABI surfaces do not expose C++ classes, STL types, exceptions, RTTI, allocator objects, or compiler object layout. _Chapters:_ 5, 9, 1, 2. _Source:_ README.md.
  • EVC-00024 specification: Fast strict is the normal development gate. Extended, release, and full proof tiers are separate gates defined by 'contracts/testing/test_tiers.contract.toml'. A green fast strict result does not claim full certification. _Chapters:_ 9, 5, 1, 2. _Source:_ README.md.
  • EVC-00025 specification: When behavior or compatibility meaning changes, update the relevant contract surface and proof in the same task. _Chapters:_ 5, 1, 2, 7. _Source:_ README.md.
  • EVC-00026 specification: Content is authored data, not runtime law. Packs and registries describe optional content, capabilities, compatibility, activation, and distribution surfaces. Missing optional content must degrade or refuse explicitly. _Chapters:_ 5, 1, 2, 3. _Source:_ README.md.
  • EVC-00028 specification: 1. Read the relevant canon, contract, and ownership documents. 2. Keep edits in the owning root. 3.
